report.task:
Design a city on a 10x10 grid. You are city planners working together.

## Grid Format
Write the result to `city.txt` — a 10x10 grid where each cell is one character,
separated by spaces, one row per line.

## Building Types
  R = Residential (houses, apartments)
  C = Commercial  (shops, offices, restaurants)
  I = Industrial  (factories, warehouses)
  P = Park        (green space, recreation)
  . = Road        (connects everything — buildings need road access)
  H = Hospital    (healthcare)
  S = School      (education)
  _ = Empty       (unused land)

## Design Goals
Build a high-quality city that scores well on ALL of these:
1. COVERAGE — use the land (empty cells score zero)
2. ACCESSIBILITY — every building should be adjacent to a road
3. ZONING — good neighbours matter! Residential near parks/schools (+), residential near industrial (-), hospitals near roads (+), etc.
4. DIVERSITY — mix of building types, not just one kind
5. CONNECTIVITY — roads should form a connected network, minimal dead-ends

## Rules
- Grid is exactly 10 rows x 10 columns
- Each cell is exactly one of: R C I P . H S _
- Roads connect the city — buildings without adjacent roads are inaccessible
- Quality is scored 0-100 on each dimension; aggregate = harmonic mean

## Example (3x3 — yours is 10x10)
```
. . .
R P C
. H .
```
This tiny city has roads on top and bottom, with residential, park, commercial,
and hospital all adjacent to roads. Good zoning: residential next to park.

## Output
Write your city grid to `city.txt`. Discuss strategy with your team first —
where to put roads, how to zone districts, how to balance coverage and quality.
The city should reflect your team's collective design decisions.
